text: Crop: Rice (Paddy). Kharif 2023-24. Production: 136.7 MT. Area: 46.3 M ha. Yield: 2,952 kg/ha. Top: West Bengal, UP, Punjab, Odisha. India 2nd largest rice producer globally after China. MSP: ₹2,183/quintal.
Crop: Wheat. Rabi 2023-24. Production: 113.3 MT (record). Area: 31.8 M ha. Yield: 3,562 kg/ha. Top: UP, Punjab, Haryana, MP, Rajasthan. India 2nd largest wheat producer. MSP: ₹2,275/quintal.

Crop: Sugarcane. 2023-24. Production: 453 MT. Area: 5.5 M ha. India: largest sugarcane producer globally. Top: UP (47% share), Maharashtra, Karnataka. FRP: ₹315/quintal. Sugar production: 33 MT. Ethanol blending: 15%.

Crop: Cotton. Kharif 2023-24. Production: 32.5 million bales. Area: 12.7 M ha. Yield: 456 kg/ha. Top: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Telangana, Rajasthan. India 3rd after China and US in production. MSP: ₹6,620/quintal (long staple).

Crop: Pulses. 2023-24. Production: 23.5 MT. Area: 30.8 M ha. Yield: 763 kg/ha. Major: Tur, Chana, Moong, Urad. India imports ~15% of pulse demand. Government push for self-sufficiency. MSP increased substantially.
Crop: Soybean. Kharif 2023-24. Production: 13.2 MT. Area: 12 M ha. India largest soybean producer in Asia outside of China. Top: MP (largest), Maharashtra, Rajasthan. MSP: ₹4,600/quintal.
Crop: Maize. 2023-24. Production: 35.6 MT. Area: 10.2 M ha. Used for animal feed (60%) and starch industry. Growing ethanol feedstock. Top: Karnataka, MP, Bihar, Tamil Nadu. MSP: ₹2,090/quintal.

Crop: Total Oilseeds. 2023-24. Production: 41 MT. Area: 29.5 M ha. Includes groundnut, mustard, soybean, sunflower. India still imports 60% edible oil needs. National Edible Oil Mission targets self-sufficiency.
